action.reasoning: Observation: evaluation still fails because model.py requires horizon_scale/horizon_bias for legacy checkpoints, while ema_weighted.pt/improved.pt do not contain them; fixed_retrain.pt also needs an end_conv variant with kernel width inferred from checkpoint. Analysis: this is purely a backward-compatibility loader issue. The fastest path is to make model construction infer optional modules from state_dict keys/shapes and load strict=True only after building the exact matching architecture. Decision: rewrite model.py to support three checkpoint families (older baseline without skip/horizon_mixer/horizon params, legacy weighted with optional horizon params, and end-conv with optional post block and variable temporal kernel), then locally smoke test all saved checkpoints and submit the best public checkpoint ema_weighted.pt once loading is verified.
